title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: See package README configuring system able run . main issue define MUP_CONDA path system (Unix/Linux) environment variable tell reticulate use environment Python initialized. can get started.
code: library(reticulate) #> Warning: package 'reticulate' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(tidyverse) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(MixedUpSlimSims) library(CKMRpop)  mup_conda <- Sys.getenv("MUP_CONDA") if (mup_conda == "") {   stop("Set MUP_CONDA to the full path of your MixedUpSlimSims conda environment.") }  reticulate::use_condaenv(mup_conda, required = TRUE) Sys.setenv(PATH = paste(file.path(mup_conda, "bin"), Sys.getenv("PATH"), sep = ":"))

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: example starts allele frequencies estimated Cyclone Creek. input file one row per marker, marker positions population-specific allele frequencies. file, column WCT column B RBT. SLiM model used , population 1 WCT-like population receiving RBT ancestry, population 2 RBT-like population. Therefore, rename frequency columns af1 af2 order. MixedUpSlimSims also needs marker’s cumulative genomic position: coordinate across whole genome, within chromosome. cumulative_genome_position() helper adds coordinates chromosome metadata. SLiM script initialize first generation VCF named slim_input.vcf. simulate 1000 diploid founder genotypes source population write format example SLiM model expects.
code: cc_freqs <- read_csv("inputs/cyclone-creek-variable-markers-freqs.csv") #> Rows: 628 Columns: 4 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (1): chrom_f #> dbl (3): pos, A, B #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. head(cc_freqs) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   chrom_f      pos        A       B #>   <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 omy01    5604542 1.00     0.0400  #> 2 omy01   15520536 0.502    0.00309 #> 3 omy01   16635910 0.493    0.367   #> 4 omy01   17490399 0.932    0.00329 #> 5 omy01   17490429 0.0657   0.00239 #> 6 omy01   18387369 0.000522 0.323 cc_freqs2 <- cc_freqs %>%   mutate(chrom = as.character(chrom_f)) %>%   select(-chrom_f) %>%   cumulative_genome_position(mykiss_chroms)   frqs <- cc_freqs2 %>%   rename(af1 = A, af2 = B, POS = cPOS) %>%   select(POS, af1, af2) simulate_founder_genos(frqs, N = c(1000, 1000), pop_names = c("wct", "rbt")) %>%   write_input_vcf(out_path = "slim_input.vcf")

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: SLiM script controls demography, migration, tree-sequence recording, individuals written VCF. Several example scripts included inst/SLiM-models; vignette uses two-population, 10-generation example. full script available GitHub: 2-pop-10-gens-vcf-initialize.slim. compatibility functions used , script needs : read founder genotypes slim_input.vcf; call initializeTreeSeq() write tree sequence named SLiM.trees; remember individuals needed ancestry tracing, especially founder individuals whose population origins define ancestry; write sampled VCFs names like p1-10.vcf, population SLiM year can parsed file name. Now run model SLiM: writes sampled VCF files years requested SLiM script: also writes SLiM.trees, records ancestry information tskit process.
code: # get the SLiM file.  It ships with the R package. file <- system.file("SLiM-models/2-pop-10-gens-vcf-initialize.slim", package = "MixedUpSlimSims") # run it via system system(paste("slim -s 333 ", file, collapse = " ")) dir(pattern = "p[0-9]+-[0-9]+\\.vcf") #> [1] "p1-10.vcf" "p1-11.vcf" "p1-9.vcf"  "p2-10.vcf" "p2-11.vcf" "p2-9.vcf"

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: SLiM time runs forward, tskit time measured backward final generation. example, SLiM year 11 tskit time 0, SLiM year 10 tskit time 1, back SLiM year 1 tskit time 10. Since 11 years simulation, retain nodes tskit times 0 10 populations 1 2. read_and_filter_trees() loads simplifies tree sequence, leaving simplified tskit tree sequence py$ts. next step turns Python nodes individuals ordinary R tibbles. founder nodes define population origin ancestry tracts. example nodes existed tskit time 10, corresponds SLiM year 1. focal nodes individuals whose ancestry want summarize. final sampled generations. , show ancestry process time, use individuals SLiM year 2 forward, tskit time 9 0, populations. Population labels coming SLiM simulation base-1 indexed, populations named 1 2 rather 0 1. get_focal_nodes() converts population-year choices node IDs needed ancestry-linking code. object fnl keeps focal nodes divided year. ancestral_segs() cycles years links cohort back founders separately. avoids problem older focal samples can become linked ancestors younger samples, excluding actual founders whose populations origin need. Now recover ancestry segments focal individuals.
code: # read the trees. Dump just captures the python code that was # evaluated to get things in the py variable in the global env. dump <- read_and_filter_trees(   trees_path = "SLiM.trees",   years_list = list(`1` = 0:10, `2` = 0:10) ) ni <- ts_nodes_and_inds(py$ts) founders <- founder_node_ids_and_pops(ni, 10) focal_tibble <- tibble(   pop = rep(c(1,2), each = 10),   time = rep(0:9, 2) )  focal_tibble #> # A tibble: 20 × 2 #>      pop  time #>    <dbl> <int> #>  1     1     0 #>  2     1     1 #>  3     1     2 #>  4     1     3 #>  5     1     4 #>  6     1     5 #>  7     1     6 #>  8     1     7 #>  9     1     8 #> 10     1     9 #> 11     2     0 #> 12     2     1 #> 13     2     2 #> 14     2     3 #> 15     2     4 #> 16     2     5 #> 17     2     6 #> 18     2     7 #> 19     2     8 #> 20     2     9 fnl <- get_focal_nodes(   ni,    Focal = focal_tibble ) segments_tib <- ancestral_segs(   ts = py$ts,   focal_nodes_list = fnl,   founder_nodes = founders$nodes,   nodes_tib = ni$nodes_tib,   indiv_tib = ni$indiv_tib )

what: Obtaining and plotting the ancestry tracts within each individual
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: previous plot shows haploid ancestry segments individual genomes. indiv_ancestry_tracts() combines two haplotypes within diploid individual, producing intervals labeled diploid ancestry state. Plot diploid ancestry tracts:  left column population 1 WCT pop . right column population 2 RBT. colors denote different ancestry combinations, coded decimal equivalents trits ternary numbers. 2 = 2 doses p1; 4 = 1 dose p1 one p2; 6 two doses p2. can extend two species.

previous_headings: Obtaining and plotting the ancestry tracts within each individual
what: How diploid ancestry states are encoded
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: ancestry state encoded ternary number, digit number copies inherited one source population. digit can 0, 1, 2. diploids, digits must sum 2. encoded value stored decimal equivalent ternary number. example, three-species case: 002: two copies species 1 — 32×0+31×0+30×2=23^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 0 + 3^0 \times 2 = 2 011: one copy species 1 one copy species 2 — 32×0+31×1+30×1=43^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 1 + 3^0 \times 1 = 4 020: two copies species 2 — 32×0+31×2+30×0=63^2 \times 0 + 3^1 \times 2 + 3^0 \times 0 = 6 200: two copies species 3 — 32×2+31×0+30×0=183^2 \times 2 + 3^1 \times 0 + 3^0 \times 0 = 18 encoding also extends naturally simulations two source populations.

what: Calculating admixture fractions from the ancestry tracts
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: diploid ancestry tracts assembled, genome-wide admixture fractions direct summary intervals.

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: Throughout package, ped_id means pedigree ID individual. SLiM’s VCF output also slimGenomePedigreeIDs field, pedigree IDs haplotypes gametes, diploid individuals. tibbles produced , ped_id, ped_p1, ped_p2 refer individual pedigree IDs. function slim_vcf2tib() reads single VCF long format. uses bcftools pipe. Long format especially space-efficient, convenient moderate-size examples used downstream tidyverse operations. Passing long = FALSE returns wide tibble instead. workflows read multiple VCF files. slim_vcfs2tib() helper lets specify populations SLiM years read. parses file names drops columns usually needed downstream. example, read SLiM years 9, 10, 11 populations p1 p2: produces large tibble, makes later filtering summarization straightforward.

what: Finding pairwise relationships among the sampled individuals
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: SLiM model kept pedigree information, can recover known relationships among sampled individuals. useful downstream tests parentage close-kin methods, , example, one might want know often aunts, uncles, siblings confused parents. package wraps relationship-finding code pairwise_relationships(). Count relationship classes found among sampled individuals: Key relationships: “avuncular”, meaning aunt-niece uncle-niece, etc. FC first cousin GP grandparent-grandchild PO1 parent-offspring Si sibling max_hit column records many ancestors shared individuals level relationship. Si max_hit = 1 half-sibling, Si max_hit = 2 full-sibling. simple Wright-Fisher example produces relatively full siblings compared half siblings. analyses need full-sibling structure, non-Wright-Fisher SLiM model can used instead.
code: pairwise_relats <- pairwise_relationships(   IT = ni$indiv_tib,   sample_years = 0:2,   num_gen = 2 ) pairwise_relats %>%   count(dom_relat, max_hit) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    dom_relat max_hit     n #>    <chr>       <int> <int> #>  1 A               1 32160 #>  2 A               2    97 #>  3 FC              1 47910 #>  4 FC              2   363 #>  5 FC              3     1 #>  6 GP              1  7982 #>  7 GP              2     9 #>  8 PO              1  8000 #>  9 Si              1 12140 #> 10 Si              2    14

title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: true ancestry tracts can also used simulate diagnostic markers. example diagnostic marker set included package data. place markers individuals last three years simulation.
code: sim_diag <- example_spp_diag_markers %>%   cumulative_genome_position(mykiss_chroms) %>%   rename(diag_spp_idx = diag_spp) %>%   place_diagnostic_markers(IndivSegs = indiv_segs %>% filter(ind_time %in% 0:2)) #> Running bedtools in /var/folders/ln/5brnn1wx0vj58fhlbbdjm8h40000gp/T//RtmpN2fTeD/file13ee22906ee81 #> Rows: 7782000 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "\t" #> chr (1): chrom #> dbl (4): diag_spp_idx, pos, ped_id, geno #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

what: Writing out those two data sets
title: Example Use of MixedUpSlimSims
text: final step prepares simulated marker data corresponding truth data downstream analyses. subsample individuals, format diagnostic markers, convert variable-marker VCF data back chromosome-specific positions, keep true metadata, ancestry fractions, ancestry tracts. object Q_values contains individual metadata, pedigree information, true admixture fractions. also keep true ancestry segments individuals included marker data. objects can written together one R object containing simulated observed data known truth:
code: set.seed(1)  subsamp <- sample(unique(sim_diag$ped_id), size = 2500, replace = FALSE)  sim_diag_ready <- sim_diag %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) %>%   rename(diag_spp = diag_spp_idx) %>%   mutate(diag_spp = case_match(diag_spp, 1 ~ "WCT", 2 ~ "RBT", 3 ~ "YCT")) %>%   rename(n = geno, indiv = ped_id) %>%   mutate(indiv = as.character(indiv), pos = as.integer(pos), n = as.integer(n))  # Convert the variable markers back to their original chromosome-specific # genome positions. cc_freqs3 <- cc_freqs2 %>%   select(pos, chrom, cPOS)  sim_var_snps_ready <- sample_genos_long %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) %>%   select(pos, ped_id, haplo, allele)  %>%   rename(cPOS = pos) %>%   left_join(cc_freqs3, by = join_by(cPOS)) %>%   select(chrom, pos, ped_id, allele) %>%   group_by(chrom, pos, ped_id) %>%   summarise(n = sum(as.integer(allele))) %>%   ungroup() %>%   arrange(ped_id, chrom, pos) %>%   rename(indiv = ped_id) %>%   mutate(indiv = as.character(indiv), pos = as.integer(pos)) #> `summarise()` has grouped output by 'chrom', 'pos'. You can override using the #> `.groups` argument. Q_values #> # A tibble: 40,000 × 9 #>    ind_id anc_pop admix_fract ind_pop ind_time   sex ped_id ped_p1 ped_p2 #>     <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>   <int>    <dbl> <int>  <int>  <int>  <int> #>  1      0       1     0             1        0     0  20000  19195  19946 #>  2      0       2     1             1        0     0  20000  19195  19946 #>  3      1       1     0             1        0     0  20001  19433  19577 #>  4      1       2     1             1        0     0  20001  19433  19577 #>  5      2       1     0.00533       1        0     0  20002  19425  19804 #>  6      2       2     0.995         1        0     0  20002  19425  19804 #>  7      3       1     0             1        0     0  20003  19195  19668 #>  8      3       2     1             1        0     0  20003  19195  19668 #>  9      4       1     0.0182        1        0     0  20004  19435  19912 #> 10      4       2     0.982         1        0     0  20004  19435  19912 #> # ℹ 39,990 more rows  true_segs <- indiv_segs %>%   filter(ped_id %in% subsamp) dir.create("results/004", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) sim_results <- list(   spp_diag = sim_diag_ready,   var_snps = sim_var_snps_ready,   metaQ = Q_values,   true_segs = true_segs,   cumul_genome = mykiss_chroms,   alle_freqs = cc_freqs ) write_rds(sim_results, file = "results/004/sim_results.rds", compress = "xz")

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Anderson EC, Grummer J (2026). MixedUpSlimSims: R Utilities Running SLiM Processing tskit Trees. R package version 0.0.1.
code: @Manual{,   title = {MixedUpSlimSims: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees},   author = {Eric C. Anderson and Jared Grummer},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1}, }

what: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: MixedUpSlimSims R package simulating admixed populations SLiM processing resulting tree sequences genotypes R. main use run SLiM simulations, simplify inspect resulting tskit tree sequences Python, return R tibbles describing ancestry tracts, ancestry fractions, diagnostic marker genotypes, pairwise relationships among simulated individuals. practice, using package means bringing demographic model written SLiM script, plus enough genetic information initialize interpret simulation. usual workflow : prepare allele frequencies source populations; simulate founder genotypes frequencies write slim_input.vcf; run SLiM script reads founder genotypes, records tree sequences, writes SLiM.trees; use MixedUpSlimSims read tree sequence, identify sampled individuals founder lineages, recover population--origin tracts, summarize genotypes ancestry. Example SLiM scripts included inst/SLiM-models; scripts work smoothly package write SLiM.trees, write sampled VCFs names like p1-10.vcf want use VCF-parsing helpers directly. functions use reticulate mostly make tskit pieces available R. example, read_and_filter_trees() loads simplifies tree sequence populations times request, ts_nodes_and_inds() turns tskit nodes individuals R tibbles, ancestral_segs() uses tskit ancestry-linking machinery recover genomic segments inherited founder populations. Higher-level helpers, especially slim_sim_a_dataset(), combine many steps, users still need provide SLiM model inputs match assumptions model. package intended installed GitHub, CRAN. deliberately uses reticulate command-line tools SLiM, bedtools, bcftools, users need set compatible conda environment using simulation tree-processing functions.

title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: Install R package GitHub:

title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: MixedUpSlimSims expects conda environment contains Python command-line dependencies used package. Create conda environment containing slim, tskit, numpy, pandas, bedtools, bcftools. recent versions work, SLiM version 4. environment can name; examples use mixed--parents. Find full path environment. Export path MUP_CONDA shell startup file normally use R sessions, ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, scheduler/cluster job script. Restart shell run source ~/.bashrc / source ~/.zshrc editing file. SEDNA: working SEDNA cluster, may also need reinstall pandas inside environment avoid system GLIBCXX error: unless get errors look like: ImportError: /lib64/libstdc++..6: version 'GLIBCXX_3.4.29' found
code: conda create -n mixed-up-parents -c conda-forge -c bioconda \     anaconda::pandas \     conda-forge::numpy \     conda-forge::slim==4.2.2 \     conda-forge::tskit  \     bedtools \     bcftools conda env list # For MixedUpSlimSims. Change this path to your own conda environment. export MUP_CONDA=/path/to/miniforge3/envs/mixed-up-parents conda activate mixed-up-parents  # activate the environment pip install pandas --no-cache-dir --force-reinstall  # reinstall pandas in there

title: R Utilities for Running SLiM and Processing tskit Trees
text: script interactive session uses Python-backed functions, set reticulate Python initialized: cluster, also fine activate conda environment starting R, MUP_CONDA still defined scripts can consistently locate environment. intentionally repository-level .Rprofile package. local .Rprofile hard-coded conda path convenient one developer fragile everyone else, R silently use wrong path project opened another machine. want local convenience settings, put user-level R startup files create untracked .Rprofile machine.
code: library(reticulate) library(MixedUpSlimSims)  mup_conda <- Sys.getenv("MUP_CONDA") if (mup_conda == "") {   stop("Set MUP_CONDA to the full path of your MixedUpSlimSims conda environment.") }  reticulate::use_condaenv(mup_conda, required = TRUE) Sys.setenv(PATH = paste(file.path(mup_conda, "bin"), Sys.getenv("PATH"), sep = ":"))

Place diagnostic marker alleles within individauls according to their ancestry haplotypes — place_diagnostic_markers
text: Markers tibble must columns: diag_spp_idx, gives 1-based index species 1 allele fixed marker (species fixed 0 allele). cPOS, positions cumulative base pairs SLiM uses. chrom: name chromosome (actual genome modeled) pos: position base pairs chromosome chrom. row tibble must specify unique species-diagnostic marker, species-diagnostic markers wish place simulated individuals included tibble. IndivSegs tibble individual segments like returned function indiv_ancestry_tracts(). Though can arbitrary columns, must include least following: ped_id: pedigree id individual left: left endpoint interval right: right endpoint interval anc1: 1-based index ancestry haplotype 1 within interval. anc2: 1-based index ancestry haplotype 2 within interval.

title: Place diagnostic marker alleles within individauls according to their ancestry haplotypes — place_diagnostic_markers
code: # Prepare Markers. Name the columns right, and get cumulative cPOS in there. M <- example_spp_diag_markers %>%   dplyr::filter(diag_spp < 3) %>%   dplyr::rename(diag_spp_idx = diag_spp) %>%   cumulative_genome_position(Cumul = mykiss_chroms)  # Now, also get some example indiv-segs. We have some as a package # data object. IS <- example_indiv_segs  # Then run the function: place_diagnostic_markers(M, IS) #> Running bedtools in /var/folders/ln/5brnn1wx0vj58fhlbbdjm8h40000gp/T//Rtmp1Hl8ZY/file13d4a5f18afa8 #> Rows: 10580 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "\t" #> chr (1): chrom #> dbl (4): diag_spp_idx, pos, ped_id, geno #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> # A tibble: 10,580 × 5 #>    diag_spp_idx chrom      pos ped_id  geno #>           <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #>  1            1 omy01  5604476  20055     2 #>  2            2 omy01  5604516  20055     0 #>  3            1 omy01  5604556  20055     2 #>  4            2 omy01  8362156  20055     0 #>  5            2 omy01  9582183  20055     0 #>  6            1 omy01 12303335  20055     2 #>  7            2 omy01 16142369  20055     0 #>  8            2 omy01 16142395  20055     0 #>  9            2 omy01 16206842  20055     0 #> 10            2 omy01 16206875  20055     0 #> # ℹ 10,570 more rows

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
code: slim_sim_a_dataset(   AF,   genome_info,   founder_pop_nums,   slim_file,   years_list,   marker_pop_time_list,   diagnostic_markers,   slim_seed = sample(1:1e+06, 1),   focal_nodes = NULL,   num_gen = 2 )

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
text: AF tibble allele frequencies columns must chrom pos, many columns wish, one holding frequency 1 allele population.  names columns give names species/subspecies. genome_info tibble cumulative base pairs different chromosomes genome giving positions AF.  necessary easily translating positions continuous coordinates SLiM.  Must columns chrom, start, end, cumul_start, cumul_end.  See mykiss_chroms example. founder_pop_nums vector number diploids founder population.  commensurate beginning population size SLiM simulation file. slim_file relative absolute path .slim file used SLiM simulation. must written creates: VCF output format "p" + subpop.id + "-" + sim.cycle + ".vcf" tree sequence recorded output named "SLiM.trees" years_list named list. name integer signifying SLiM population, value integer vector tree-seq years individuals/nodes tree seq named population retained tree-sequence. See documentation read_and_filter_trees() information.  must concordant SLiM file used. marker_pop_time_list list pops years extract genotypes VCF files.  named list names names population "p1", "p2", etc. years SLiM years (get higher later time) samples desired.  example: list(p1 = 9:11, p2 = 9:11). diagnostic_markers tibble columns diag_spp, chrom, pos. values diag_spp must include species codes used column headers AF. slim_seed single integer seed use SLiM.  Gets set automatically R RNG set. focal_nodes tibble specifying populations (base-1 indexed) cohorts (base-0 tskit time—recent 0) want determine ancestry vectors.  left NULL done populations non-founder generations. num_gen number generations back trace relationships individuals.  default 2, get parent-offspring, full- half siblings, grandparent/grandchild, aunts uncles first cousins.

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
text: Note also currently configured infers founder generation' (tskit time) time smallest ped_id existed.

title: Simulate a complete data set to run in MixedUpParents — slim_sim_a_dataset
code: AF <- cyclone_creek_var_freqs genome_info <- mykiss_chroms founder_pop_nums <- c(1000, 1000) slim_file <- system.file(   "SLiM-models/2-pop-10-gens-vcf-initialize.slim",   package = "MixedUpSlimSims" ) slim_seed <- 55 years_list <- list(`1` = 0:10, `2` = 0:10) marker_pop_time_list <- list(p1 = 9:11, p2 = 9:11) diagnostic_markers <- wct_rbt_yct_diagnostic_markers  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # this takes more time than CRAN likes, so don't run it on Check simmed <- slim_sim_a_dataset(   AF = AF,   genome_info = genome_info,   founder_pop_nums = founder_pop_nums,   slim_file = slim_file,   slim_seed = slim_seed,   years_list = years_list,   marker_pop_time_list = marker_pop_time_list,   diagnostic_markers = diagnostic_markers )  } # }

what: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
text: simulated SLIM dataset slim_sim_a_dataset(), can use function following: Downsample consistent subsample number NV variable markers /number ND diagnostic ones. Create missing data remaining markers simple rate MV MD across variable diagnostic markers, respectively. Randomly toss individual sample sample single rate S across individuals, pops times. Downsample number individuals specific number fraction population time (yet implemented).

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
code: tweak_slim_simmed_data(   X,   NV = NA,   ND = NA,   S = NA,   MV = NA,   MD = NA,   EV = NA,   ED = NA )

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
text: X return object slim_sim_a_dataset(). NV number variable markers randomly downsample , retaining markers across individuals completely discarding rest. NV greater total number variable markers warning issued full set markers returned. ND number diagnostic markers randomly downsample , retaining markers across individuals completely discarding rest. ND greater total number diagnostic markers warning issued full set markers returned. S fraction sampled individuals randomly retained.  , want sampled 40% population set S = 0.4. MV missing data data rate variable markers. genotype variable-marker data set (subsampling NV markers) independent probability MV missing applied. MD missing data data rate diagnostic markers. genotype variable-marker data set (subsampling NV markers) independent probability MV missing applied. EV probability non-missing variable marker genotype individual genotyping error.  genotype simulated genotyping error, true genotype 1 choose either 0 1, probability 1/2. true genotype 0 2, genotyping error results 1.  (Genotyping error opposite homozygote occur). ED probability non-missing diagnostic marker genotypes individual genotyping error.  genotype simulated genotyping error, true genotype 1 choose either 0 1, probability 1/2. true genotype 0 2, genotyping error results 1.  (Genotyping error opposite homozygote occur).

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
text: returns list downsampled genotyping-errored versions data set.

title: Subsample SNPs or individuals and simulated genotyping error — tweak_slim_simmed_data
code: # get a data set to work with X <- slim_sim_dataset_example  tweaked <- tweak_slim_simmed_data(   X,   NV = 600,   ND = 1000,   S = 0.7,   MV = 0.1,   MD = 0.15,   EV = 0.01,   ED = 0.005 )
